results.description: DESCRIPTION:THIS PROJECT AIMS TO EVALUATE SEVERAL METHODS OF MODIFICATION OF RURAL BASINS TO ENHANCE RECHARGE WHILE MONITORING THE EFFICACY OF EACH APPROACH.ACTIVITIES:IN PARTNERSHIP WITH THE LOCAL STAKEHOLDERS AND THE SITE OWNER (CITY OF ADA, OK), THIS PROJECT WILL EMPLOY MICROBIOLOGICAL, GEOCHEMICAL, SEDIMENTOLOGICAL, GROUNDWATER GAS MONITORING, GEOPHYSICAL, AND GROUNDWATER MODELING TOOLS TO TEST THE EFFECTIVENESS AND IMPACTS OF RURAL EAR STRUCTURES TO DETERMINE IF THESE STRUCTURES CAN BE DEPLOYED AS PART OF RURAL LAND MANAGEMENT STRATEGIES TO SAFELY INCREASE GROUNDWATER SUPPLIES IN FRACTURED AND KARSTIC AQUIFERS.SUBRECIPIENT:TEXAS STATE UNIVERSITY ($443,592) WILL CONTRIBUTE TO THE GROUNDWATER MODEL AND GAS TRANSPORT DEVELOPMENT (TASK E), INCLUDING TRAVEL TO RESEARCH SITES AND PROJECT MEETINGS. EAST CENTRAL UNIVERSITY OKA INSTITUTE ($294,482) WILL CONTRIBUTE TO THE COLLABORATION AND ENGAGEMENT PLAN, HOST THE STAKEHOLDER MEETINGS, AND MANAGE THE MARKETING AND EDUCATIONAL PRODUCT DEVELOPMENT.OUTCOMES:DELIVERABLES INCLUDE A GUIDANCE DOCUMENT FOR THE SITING AND ASSESSMENT OF RURAL EAR AS WELL AS A LITERATURE REVIEW AND MONITORING TOOLS DEVELOPED TO MANAGE RECHARGE PULSES THROUGH FRACTURED AND KARSTIC AQUIFERS, AND GUIDANCE ON MONITORING BIOGEOCHEMICAL CHANGES. THE OUTCOMES INCLUDE PROVIDING LAND AND WATER MANAGERS THE ABILITY TO SAFELY AND ECONOMICALLY IMPLEMENT RURAL EAR. THE BENEFICIARIES INCLUDE RESIDENTS OF OKLAHOMA WHOSE WATER SUPPLY COMES FROM ARBUCKLE SIMPSON AQUIFER, LOCAL AND TRIBAL GOVERNMENT, INDUSTRIAL AND AGRICULTURAL USERS, THE AQUATIC ENVIRONMENT OF CONNECTED STREAMS, AND THE GENERAL PUBLIC.
